seoTitle: How to Export Jira Projects to Microsoft Excel
metaDescription: Export an entire Jira project's issues into Microsoft Excel for reporting, archiving, or sharing with non-Jira users.
intro: Not everyone who needs project data has a Jira license, and Excel remains the easiest way to share that information broadly. This guide covers exporting a full project's worth of issues into Microsoft Excel.

steps.heading: Search for all issues in the project
steps.body: Use a project-based filter in Jira's issue search to pull together every issue belonging to that project.

steps.heading: Refine the fields you need
steps.body: Adjust the columns shown in the search results to match what should appear in the final Excel file.

steps.heading: Open the export menu
steps.body: From the search results screen, find the export option near the top of the issue list.

steps.heading: Choose the Excel format
steps.body: Select Excel (or CSV) as the export type instead of other formats like Word or PDF.

steps.heading: Download and share the file
steps.body: Save the exported file and share it with stakeholders who need the data outside of Jira.

faq.question: Can I export a project's data on a recurring basis?
faq.answer: Native exports are manual each time, though the Jira REST API can be used to automate recurring exports if needed.

faq.question: Will the export include attachments and comments?
faq.answer: Standard issue exports to Excel typically include field data only, not attachments, and comments may require a separate export method.

faq.question: Is there a size limit on how many issues I can export?
faq.answer: Very large projects may hit export limits, requiring the data to be pulled in smaller batches.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to export a whole project?
faq.answer: You generally just need view access to the project's issues, though visibility can be restricted by project permissions.
